sections.name: 2.1 Tone & Voice Calibration
sections.content:
| Dimension | L2 Setting |
|---|---|
| Hope/Realism Ratio | 40/60 — realism-dominant, cracked hope, science-anchored |
| Urgency | LOW — no Pre-K framing, no deadlines, no "act now" |
| Empathy Intensity | HIGH — validates exhaustion, names specific failed methods |
| Science Density | HIGH — interoception detail, Nicholson 2019 citation, mechanism depth |
| BCBA Positioning | Complement frame — BCBA built the behavioral foundation, BSL adds sensory layer. Aggressive BCBA criticism = FORBIDDEN |
| CTA Aggressiveness | LOW — "When you're ready" / "60 days by your judgment" |
| Decision Period | 7–21 days — this email is one data point in her research process |
| Sentence Style | Measured, deliberate, evidence-based — clinical warmth |
| Email Length | Long (600–800 words) — L2 reads deeply, scans for mechanism specificity |

sections.name: 2.2 The 6-Element Formula Compliance Check
sections.content:
| Element | Where It Appears |
|---|---|
| ① Real Symptom Recognition | ¶2-3 — Named 8 specific failed methods (BCBA protocol, OT, social stories, sticker charts, timer sits, naked weekend, juice loading, reward systems, private consultant). Tests "he sits when prompted but never initiates" — the L2 marker |
| ② Mechanism Specificity | ¶4-9 — Interoception defined, Nicholson 2019 cited by name, "signal from bladder to brain" language, prompt-dependency explained as neurological consequence, not training failure |
| ③ BCBA/OT Acknowledge | ¶6-8 — "Your BCBA has spent 3–5 years building critical skills," "That foundation matters," complement frame repeated twice, never criticizes BCBA work |
| ④ Realistic Timelines | ¶9 — "4–8 weeks for first signals, 12+ for others," "progress isn't linear," "regression is recalibration, not starting over" |
| ⑤ 60-Day Guarantee | ¶10 — "Try for 60 days," "If nothing changes, full refund," "No questions, no hoops" |
| ⑥ Permission-Not-to-Buy | ¶11-12 — "If this feels like one more thing... the product will be here next month," explicit permission to wait, BCBA trust recalibration acknowledged |

Validation: All 6 elements present. Conversion viability confirmed.

sections.name: 2.3 Subject Line Strategy
sections.content:
Primary: "Why every method failed, neurology, not effort" (Mechanism tease with science anchor, 44 chars)
Alt A: "Your BCBA built the foundation. This adds what's missing." (Complement frame, 58 chars)
Alt B: "Nicholson 2019 explains why the signal never arrived" (Science citation, 53 chars)

Rules followed:
- Mechanism tease with science anchor ✓
- NO urgency cues ✓
- NO Pre-K reference ✓
- Designed for 7–21 day decision window ✓

sections.name: 2.4 CTA Architecture
sections.content:
| Section | CTA Type | Placement |
|---|---|---|
| After mechanism deep-dive | Text link — "Read the Nicholson 2019 study summary" | End of mechanism section |
| After timeline | Text link — "See what 4–8 week progress actually looks like" | After realistic timeline |
| Final | Muted green button — "When you're ready — Learn about the 3+3 Bundle" | After permission-not-to-buy |
| P.S. | Text link — "Reply to get the BCBA fact sheet" | Post-script |

CTA Intensity: LOW across all placements. No urgency framing. No limited availability. No countdown.

sections.name: 2.5 Visual & Design Strategy (L2-Calibrated)
sections.content:
Color Palette (Muted/L2):
- Brand-Green #039902, used sparingly, desaturated to ~70% opacity for background elements
- Cream #FBF7EB, dominant background, warmth without intensity
- Warm Gray #9AA8A7, secondary text, supporting elements
- Soft Sage #C5D5C7, accent borders, dividers
- Mint #DBFFCD, very sparingly, 1–2 highlight elements max
- NO bright yellows or high-saturation accents, L2 responds to clinical calm

Typography:
- Font: Questrial weight 400 only
- Body: 16px, leading 1.6
- Subheadings: 20px, tracking 0.02em
- Pull quotes: 18px italic, Cream background with soft sage left border
- NO decorative fonts, NO bold weights except for the mechanism framing sentence

Imagery Guidelines (BCBA-Aware, Science-Visual):
1. Hero image (top of email): Soft-focus image of a child (age 5–7, mixed verbal profile) sitting in a calm, familiar environment, home bathroom or therapy room corner. Warm natural light. No direct eye contact. Authentic posture, not staged. Child engaged in everyday activity, not potty-focused.
2. Mechanism section: Clean, simple diagram, brain ↔ bladder communication pathway with a visual "gap" indicator. Desaturated warm gray and cream tones. Explicitly NOT cartoonish or oversimplified. A visual that could sit in a clinical handout.
3. BCBA complement section: Optional second image, therapist/parent-child interaction, side-by-side. Warm, collaborative feel. Avoid over-promising body language (no child-on-toilet victory shots).
4. Guarantee/permission section: No imagery, white space is the design element here. Creates a pause in the reading rhythm.
5. Imagery forbidden: Staged toilet-sitting photos, celebration shots, before/after comparison images, bright stock photography, cartoon illustrations of body functions, neurotypical child comparisons.

Layout Structure:
```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ [Brand-Green header line, narrow] │
│ [Hero image, warm, natural light] │
│ [Subject line repeated as H1, Cream bg] │
│ │
│ Body paragraphs, warm gray on Cream │
│ [Soft Sage divider] │
│ "Your child's brain never received │
│ the message.", Pull quote in Cream card │
│ [Soft Sage divider] │
│ Body paragraphs continue │
│ [Diagram, brain-bladder signal gap] │
│ Body paragraphs continue │
│ [Soft Sage divider] │
│ BCBA complement section │
│ [Image, therapist/parent-child] │
│ Timeline section, bullet-free, paragraph │
│ [White space] │
│ Guarantee section │
│ [White space] │
│ Permission section │
│ [Button, muted green] │
│ [Lena sign-off block, Cream bg] │
│ [P.S., BCBA fact sheet offer] │
│ [Brand-Green footer line, narrow] │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

Whitespace Strategy: Minimum 24px between sections. 40px before guarantee and permission sections. These are the "breathing room" moments, L2 needs space to think, not be pushed.

sections.name: 2.6 Proof Point Distribution
sections.content:
| Proof Type | Location | L2 Strength |
|---|---|---|
| Nicholson et al. (2019) citation | Mechanism section — named study | ★★★★★ — Highest L2 lever |
| 8 specific failed methods named | Symptom recognition section | ★★★★★ — Trust builder |
| BCBA complement frame | Dedicated section + throughout | ★★★★★ — Gatekeeper neutralizer |
| Realistic timeline (4–8 / 12+ weeks) | Timeline section | ★★★★ — Skepticism counter |
| 60-day guarantee | Guarantee section | ★★★★ — Risk removal |
| "Your BCBA has spent 3–5 years" | BCBA section | ★★★★ — Respect frame |
| Prompt-dependency explanation | Mechanism section | ★★★★ — Insight hook |
| "Hundreds of L2 families" | Timeline section | ★★★ — Social proof (subtle) |
| Neurological explanation for regression | Timeline section | ★★★ — Fear reduction |

Note: No peer-mom testimonial in this email, E1 is pure mechanism and trust foundation. Testimonials are introduced in E2 (D29). E1 establishes that she is being spoken to as an intelligent evaluator of evidence, not as a buyer.

sections.name: 2.7 Objections Pre-Emptively Handled
sections.content:
| Objection | Where Handled | How |
|---|---|---|
| D1: "I've tried everything" | ¶2-3 — Lists 8+ specific failed methods, then reframes: "You didn't fail. The problem was the assumption." |
| B2: "My BCBA has a protocol" | ¶6-8 — Dedicated BCBA complement section. Never criticizes. Positions BSL as additive layer. |
| O5: "Regression after success" | ¶9 — Named explicitly. Reframed as "recalibration, not restart." |
| D3: "What if I get my hopes up?" | ¶11-12 — Permission-not-to-buy. "The product will be here next month." |
| C3: "Worth the money?" | Not addressed — E1 is education-only. Financial framing comes in E3 (D36). |

sections.name: 2.8 Lena Voice Compliance Check
sections.content:
| Rule | Status |
|---|---|
| Peer-to-peer, never founder framing | ✅ "I hear this list from L2 parents every day" |
| Every mail ends with Lena sign-off | ✅ "— Lena" with profile subtitle |
| Never sound like marketing | ✅ Clinical warmth, no superlatives, no "amazing", no "game-changing" |
| Never "easy" / "simple" / "quickly" / "guaranteed" / "miracle" / "cure" / "fix" / "solve" | ✅ None present |
| Never compare to neurotypical kids as standard | ✅ No NT comparisons |
| Always validate failed attempts before proposing mechanism | ✅ ¶2-3 = method validation → ¶4 = mechanism |
| Pull-ups are a tool that blocks signal — not "bad" | ✅ Pull-ups not mentioned in E1 (reserved for E3) |
| Autism is identity, not disease | ✅ Language throughout affirms neurology, not pathology |

sections.name: 2.9 Forbidden Language Check (L2-Specific)
sections.content:
| Phrase | Check |
|---|---|
| "Quick" | ❌ Not present |
| "Easy" | ❌ Not present |
| "Just like other kids" | ❌ Not present |
| Pre-K urgency | ❌ Not present |
| Aggressive BCBA criticism | ❌ Not present — dedicated complement frame |
| "Don't give up!" | ❌ Not present |
| "Magic" / "Miracle" | ❌ Not present |
| "We can train X weeks" | ❌ Not present — realistic timeline instead |
| Specific timeline promise | ❌ Not present — "4-8 weeks for first signals, 12+ for others" |

sections.name: 2.10 Science Citations
sections.content:
| Citation | Use | Confidence |
|---|---|---|
| Nicholson et al. (2019) — J Autism Dev Dis, 49(9):3625-3637 | Core mechanism evidence: diminished body awareness in autistic children | HIGH — Landmark study |
| Hample, Mahler & Amspacher (2020) — 8-week pilot | Referenced indirectly via "interoception can be trained" | MEDIUM — N=8 pilot, reserved for E2 deep dive |

sections.name: 2.11 Mobile Rendering Notes
sections.content:
- Single column layout throughout
- CTA button: min 48px height, full-width on mobile (320px), centered on desktop
- Body text: 16px minimum, never smaller
- Pull quote card: full-bleed on mobile, padded on desktop
- Diagram: responsive SVG or stacked description, never wider than 320px on mobile
- Images: max-width 100%, height auto
- Touch targets: minimum 44×44px for all links and buttons
- Line length on desktop: capped at 600px for readability

sections.name: 2.12 Success Metrics
sections.content:
| Metric | Target | Why |
|---|---|---|
| Click rate | 3–5% | L2 is slow decision — E1 click establishes trust, not conversion |
| Reply rate (P.S. BCBA sheet) | 0.5–1% | High-value signal — BCBA engagement = serious consideration |
| Open rate | 35–45% | L2 reads deeply from familiar senders (Lena) |
| Unsubscribe rate | < 0.3% | High science density can trigger overwhelm — monitor closely |
| Conversion (by E5) | 8–15% | L2 funnel: E1 is ground-layer for 7–21 day decision cycle |

---

S2 Section Reference: §1.19, PP-Extended Ed L2 E1
